data.content:
The file is generated successfully (`obj/Debug/net8.0/Generated/GeneratedInfo.cs`), but it's never added to the `Compile` item group, so the compiler doesn't see it.

**Fix:** Add a `<Compile Include>` for the generated file inside the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

data.reasoningText:
The issue is clear. The `GenerateSampleCode` target runs `BeforeTargets="CoreCompile;BeforeCompile"` and generates the file, but it doesn't add the generated file to the `Compile` item group. The file is written to disk but MSBuild doesn't know to include it in compilation.

The fix is to add a `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` item after the file is generated, within the target.

arguments.new_str:
<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>"}

<Message Importance="high" Text="Generated code file: $(_GeneratedFilePath)" />

    <!-- Include the generated file in compilation -->
    <ItemGroup>
      <Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />
    </ItemGroup>

data.content:
Build succeeds and runs correctly.

**Root cause:** The `GenerateSampleCode` target wrote the `.cs` file to disk but never added it to the `<Compile>` item group. MSBuild only compiles files it knows about through items, so the generated file was silently ignored.

**Fix:** Added `<Compile Include="$(_GeneratedFilePath)" />` inside the target's `<ItemGroup>` so the generated file is included in compilation.
